vague_intent: Every day at 9am, fetch the top 5 Hacker News stories and show them to me as a digest in the app titlebar. No need to publish anywhere else.
true_intent: Exactly as stated: a daily 9am digest of the top 5 Hacker News stories delivered to the in-app titlebar. No external publishing, no review step, no memory — it is a read-only informational report.
notes: NEGATIVE CONTROL for over-asking. Matches the simple-periodic-report fast-path (session_prompt Rule 26: schedule cadence + informational output + no external publishing => ZERO clarifying questions). Measured baseline asked 4 gratuitous questions (output format, memory, review, storage) — this scenario is the regression gate for that.

vague_intent: Every weekday at 9am, collect yesterday's merged pull requests from our repo and post a standup summary to Slack.
true_intent: A weekday 9am standup listing yesterday's merged PRs from the acme/backend GitHub repo, posted to the #standup Slack channel. The only things I did not state are WHICH repo and WHICH channel.
must_not_assume: a specific Slack channel without asking
notes: CALIBRATION control. Trigger, source type and destination PLATFORM are all specified — the build must not re-ask those, only the two concrete identifiers. Tests 'ask only what is genuinely missing'.

vague_intent: post updates about our product launches so the team stays in the loop
true_intent: When we publish a new product launch page, post a short summary into our team's Notion 'Launches' database. The destination is Notion, NOT a chat tool. The trigger is a launch being published, not a schedule.
must_clarify: what counts as a product launch
must_not_assume: a schedule trigger instead of an event trigger
notes: ASSUMPTION TRAP. 'post updates ... team stays in the loop' strongly baits Slack; the real destination is Notion. A build that binds Slack without asking fails must_not_assume.

vague_intent: make me a bot that helps with my emails
true_intent: I run customer support out of a Microsoft Outlook mailbox (NOT Gmail). Read incoming support mail, summarise each, label the urgent ones, and draft replies for the top few — but I always review before anything is sent. Nothing auto-sends.
must_clarify: whether replies auto-send or need review
notes: CONNECTOR-CHOICE + SAFETY. The measured baseline silently bound gmail_search without ever asking the provider. Also carries the auto-send review gate — the most safety-critical dimension the build must not assume away.

vague_intent: Watch the acme/backend repo on GitHub. When an issue is opened with the 'security' label, alert me in the app.
true_intent: Exactly that: watch acme/backend on GitHub for newly-opened issues labelled 'security', and raise an in-app notification. GitHub is explicitly named — there is nothing to choose.
must_not_assume: asking which source-control connector when GitHub was explicitly named
a schedule trigger instead of an event trigger
notes: DON'T-ASK oracle. The connector is named in the intent, so a connector_category question here is over-asking. Also asserts the event (not schedule) trigger.

vague_intent: manage my whole workflow and make my life easier
true_intent: Honestly I have one concrete pain: each morning I copy yesterday's Stripe payouts into a Google Sheet by hand. Automate exactly that — pull yesterday's Stripe payouts and append them to my 'Revenue' sheet each morning. Nothing else.
must_clarify: which specific job to start with
must_not_assume: inventing a broad multi-capability do-everything persona instead of asking to narrow scope
notes: SCOPE CONTROL. Maximally open intent invites the model to invent 5+ vague capabilities. A correct build asks the user to narrow to one concrete job. max_capabilities=2 is the scope-creep gate.

vague_intent: sync my tasks between my two tools
true_intent: When I add or complete a task in Todoist, mirror that change into a Notion 'Tasks' database. It is ONE-WAY, Todoist to Notion — Notion is only a readable mirror.
must_clarify: sync direction (one-way vs two-way, which is source)
must_not_assume: two-way sync when the user wants one-way
specific tools without asking which two
notes: MULTI-SLOT disambiguation. Neither endpoint is named and the direction is under-specified; assuming two-way is a real correctness risk.

recipe_name: No topic, no cadence, no output
vague_intent: I want something for my research
true_intent: I research battery chemistry. Once a week, search the web and arXiv for new papers on solid-state batteries, summarise the genuinely new ones, and save the summaries to a Notion database for later review.
notes: The topic is user-supplied content the build cannot possibly guess — a pure ASK gate. Must open with a mission question.

vague_intent: build an agent for my sales team
true_intent: When a deal is marked Closed-Won in our CRM, post a celebration to the #wins channel and append a row to our 'Closed Deals' spreadsheet with deal name, amount and owner. That is the whole job.
must_not_assume: a specific CRM or chat tool without asking
notes: Goal-only with an audience but no job. High risk of inventing a broad multi-capability 'sales assistant' instead of asking to narrow.

vague_intent: keep me updated on tech news
true_intent: Summarise the biggest AI and startup news twice a week — Monday and Thursday mornings — pulled from the web, delivered as an in-app notification. Read-only, nothing to publish.
notes: GATE-BOUNDARY probe. Sits just outside the schedule-keyword set in gates.rs (no 'daily/every/each'), so intent_implies_trigger should be Closed and force a cadence question. Contrast with ctl-hn-digest-zero-ask, which is fully inside it.
